stage1.why_it_matters_short: Helps opening hands, brushing and early grabs feel playful.
stage1.why_it_matters_long: Your baby's hands may be opening more often, brushing objects, watching fingers and starting the first accidental grabs. Soft textures, easy holds and clean objects give them something simple to notice with their hands, eyes and mouth.

stage2.mapping_rationale: Mapped from current age-band evidence into the things that can help lane.

top_picks.title: Easy-Grasp Oball
top_picks.description: A flexible, lightweight ball with 32 finger holes, perfect for tiny hands to grip, roll, and throw. Its soft construction and easy-care material make it ideal for early playtime.
top_picks.why_pip_picked_this: Excellent design for early grasping with numerous finger holes, promoting fine motor skills and sensory development. High rating and review count indicate strong retail proof.

evidence.claim: 4.9 rating, 72 reviews, £3.99 price, suitable from birth

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A lightweight Oball Classic with many soft finger holes, easy for tiny hands to grip, roll and mouth during short supervised floor play.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Reaching is just waking up at one to three months. Lots of holes mean a brush of fingers can become a hold without precise aim. Soft plastic survives the chew that follows, and it rolls gently when they finally tip it.

top_picks.title: Multi-sensory Teether
top_picks.description: An award-winning teether and rattle with a mesmerizing maze of soft, continuous, BPA-free loops that are easy for tiny hands to grasp, hold, and chew. Can be refrigerated for added soothing.
top_picks.why_pip_picked_this: Unique design with bendable loops makes it exceptionally easy for infants to grasp and manipulate, supporting fine motor skills and providing teething relief.
top_picks.founder_lens: Price is based on US site, approximate UK price from search. Need to verify exact UK price and retailer.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A Manhattan Toy Winkel with soft bendable rings that double as a rattle and teether, easy for small hands to grab, hold and chew under watch.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Rigid toys miss when fists are still clumsy. Soft bendy rings give several places to catch hold, then something to gum when drool starts. Chill it briefly if gums are sore, and keep play on the mat with you nearby.

top_picks.best_for: Sensory stimulation and fine motor skills
top_picks.title: Freddie the Firefly
top_picks.description: A friendly firefly with a soft velour body, bright colors, patterns, and textures. Features a peek-a-boo mirror, squeaker, clinking rings, and a tethered ladybug teether. Easily clips to strollers and carriers.
top_picks.why_pip_picked_this: Multi-sensory features (crinkle, squeaker, teether, mirror, varied textures) engage multiple senses and encourage reaching, grasping, and tactile exploration. Clip-on design adds versatility.
top_picks.founder_lens: Rating and review count not found on John Lewis page. Need to confirm if available elsewhere.
safety_notes: Remove all packaging and tags before giving to a child

evidence.claim: Product details, £14.99 price, suitable from birth

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A Lamaze Freddie the Firefly soft toy with crinkle, squeaker, rings, a small mirror and a tethered teether, plus a clip for pram or carrier.
top_picks.ember_verdict: When reaching starts, variety helps more than another identical ball. Textures and a soft sound give them reasons to stretch a hand. Clip it for awake pram time, then unclip for sleep so nothing hangs in the cot overnight either.

top_picks.title: Newborn Activity Kit
top_picks.description: A 12-piece kit designed to meet newborns' developmental needs, encouraging sensory exploration and tummy time. Includes flash cards, a baby-safe mirror, tummy time pillow, rattle ring, crinkling lion cub, and a soft lion blankie.
top_picks.why_pip_picked_this: Offers a comprehensive set of developmental toys for newborns, covering visual, auditory, and tactile stimulation, as well as supporting tummy time and early grasping.
top_picks.founder_lens: Review count is low (5 reviews). Need to check if more reviews are available on other retailers.

evidence.claim: 5.0 rating, 5 reviews, £32.99 price, suitable from birth

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A Taf Toys Hello Baby newborn kit with flash cards, a baby safe mirror, tummy time pillow, rattle ring, crinkle cub and soft blankie for early floor play.
top_picks.ember_verdict: If the toy drawer is empty, a kit covers the first floor jobs in one go. Mirror and cards feed the face focus, while the soft grab pieces wait for early reaches. Use pieces one at a time so the mat does not turn into clutter.

top_picks.best_for: Tactile exploration and motor skill development
top_picks.title: Soft Grasping Ball
top_picks.description: A beautifully soft grasping ball with varied textures and subtle colors, designed to support sensory exploration and motor skills from birth. Easy for little hands to grab, roll, and explore.
top_picks.why_pip_picked_this: Soft fabric and varied textures provide gentle tactile stimulation, while its design encourages grasping, rolling, and motor skill development.
top_picks.founder_lens: Rating and review count not found on Scandiborn page. Need to confirm if available elsewhere.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A Little Dutch fabric grasping ball with mixed textures and soft colours, shaped for little hands to grab, roll and explore from the earliest weeks.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Some parents prefer fabric over plastic for the first holds. Mixed textures invite a second grab once the first squeeze works. Keep colours gentle for now and save louder toys for when reaching is stronger and floor play lasts longer.

longlist.reason: Lightweight, 32 finger holes, easy to grip for small hands, excellent for early grasping.
Movement and surprise appearance engage baby, encourages reaching.

longlist.product_name: My First Gift Set New Baby Toys
longlist.reason: Likely includes graspable items for newborns, good for early development.
Encourages reaching and grasping, versatile for attachment to various baby gear.

skips.reason: Too expensive and subscription-based, also not a single 'reach-and-grab' toy but a kit.
Primarily a soother, not a reach-and-grab toy.
More suitable for 6 months+ for stacking, not ideal for 1-3 month old grasping.
Primarily a tummy time toy, not focused on reach and grab.
More of a soft doll, not specifically designed for early grasping or sensory exploration for 1-3 months.
More suitable for 6 months+ for stacking, not ideal for 1-3 month old grasping.
